zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare於1290年出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達卡斯特萊托時，他的父親John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare40歲，母親Blanche de la Roche35歲。他於1312年8月16日與Joan de Burgh在愛爾蘭庫迪 唐格林卡斯特爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1328年4月9日在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達梅諾斯享年38歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 吉爾達吉爾達。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare于1290年出生在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達卡斯特萊托时，他的父亲John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare40岁，母亲Blanche de la Roche35岁。他于1312年8月16日与Joan de Burgh在爱尔兰庫迪 唐格林卡斯特爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1328年4月9日在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達梅諾斯享年38岁，埋葬在爱尔兰庫迪 吉爾達吉爾達。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Gerald
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Geraldo
meanings: Portuguese and Spanish: from the personal name Geraldo (see Gerald ).

variants.name: Gerard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerrald
meanings: Altered form of   Gerald  .

variants.name: Giraud
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Alain, Emile, Franck, Herve, Laurent, Marie-Noelle, Sylvain.

French: from the personal name Giraud, a variant of Géraud, from the ancient Germanic name Gerwald, Girwald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’ (see also Gerald ). This surname is also found in southern Germany (see also Shero ) and in England (mainly Kent, of Huguenot origin). Compare Girod and also Gerow .

History: Étienne Giraud dit Brindamour from Vouneuil-sous-Biard in Vienne, France, married Marie Berthiaume in Montreal, QC, in 1760. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (along with its altered form Gerow) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Geralds
meanings: English: variant of Gerald , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Garn
meanings: English (western): from a lost place called Garn in Westbury on Severn (Gloucestershire).

English: perhaps a shortened form of the Old French personal name Geron, the oblique case form of Garo, a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Ger- (see Gerald , Gerard , Garbutt ). A shortened form Gern would have given rise to a variant pronunciation Garn in late Middle English.
English: perhaps a shortened form of   Garron  .

variants.name: Garrett
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Gerad, a variant of either Gerald or Gerard .

variants.name: Giraldo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luis, Carlos, Jose, Jorge, Blanca, Fernando, Gustavo, Hernan, Juan, Luz, Alberto, Diego.  Portuguese Albeiro, Ligia.
Spanish, Portuguese, and Italian: from the ancient Germanic personal nameGiraldo, equivalent to Gerald .

variants.name: Grau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Fritz, Heinz, Horst, Dieter, Frieda, Gerhard, Herta, Joerg, Johann, Manfred.
German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from German grau ‘gray’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).
Catalan and southern French: from a shortened form of the medieval personal name Guerau, a cognate of Gerald .

variants.name: Gerold
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Christien, Pierre.
English (London): variant of   Gerald  .
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerwald, composed of the elements gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’ + wald ‘rule’. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Either a short form of any of the numerous names representing a transferred use of a surname formed with the Anglo-Norman French prefix fi(t)z ‘son of ’, as for example Fitzgerald , Fitzroy , or a transferred use of the surname Fitz, which may be of the same derivation, but applied originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same given name, or a local name from Fitz in Shropshire, named with the Old English personal name Fitt + hōh ‘hill spur’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare was born in 1290, in Castletown, County Kildare, Ireland, his father, John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare, was 40 and his mother, Blanche de la Roche, was 35. He married Joan de Burgh on 16 August 1312, in Greencastle, County Down, Ireland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 9 April 1328, in Maynooth, County Kildare, Ireland, at the age of 38, and was buried in Kildare, County Kildare, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare est né en 1290, à Castletown, County Kildare, Irlande, son père, John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Blanche de la Roche, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Burgh le 16 août 1312, à Greencastle, County Down, Irlande. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 9 avril 1328, à Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlande, à l’âge de 38 ans, et a été enterré à Kildare, County Kildare, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare 1290 in Castletown, County Kildare, Irland geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare 40 und seine Mutter Blanche de la Roche 35. Er hat am 16. August 1312 in Greencastle, County Down, Irland Joan de Burgh geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 9. April 1328 im Alter von 38 Jahren in Maynooth, County Kildare, Irland und wurde in Kildare, County Kildare, Irland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare è nato il nel 1290 nel Castletown, County Kildare, Irlanda, il suo padre, John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare, aveva 40 anni e il suo madre, Blanche de la Roche, aveva 35 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Burgh il 16 agosto 1312, nel Greencastle, County Down, Irlanda. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 9 aprile 1328, nel Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlanda, all'età di 38 anni, e fu sepolto a Kildare, County Kildare, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildareが1290年にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアカストレトウンで生まれた時，父John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildareは40歳，母Blanche de la Rocheは35歳であった。1312年8月16日にアイルランドコウントイ ダウングレエンカストルでJoan de Burghと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1328年4月9日にアイルランドコウントイ キルダアメイノオスで38歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ キルダアキルダアにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare(이)가 1290년 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 카스틀레타운에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Blanche de la Roche(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1312년 8월 16일 아일랜드 카운티 다운 그린카스틀에서 Joan de Burgh(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1328년 4월 9일 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 메이노스에서 38세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 킬더르 킬더르에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare nasceu em 1290, em Castletown, County Kildare, Irlanda, seu pai, John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Blanche de la Roche, tinha 35 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Burgh em 16 de agosto de 1312, em Greencastle, County Down, Irlanda. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 9 de abril de 1328, em Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlanda, com 38 anos, e foi sepultado em Kildare, County Kildare, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare родился в 1290 г. в местности кастлетоун, коунти каилдар, Ирландия, его отцу по имени John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Blanche de la Roche, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Burgh 16 августа 1312 г. в местности гринкастл, коунти доун, Ирландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 9 апреля 1328 г. в местности меинут, коунти каилдар, Ирландия в возрасте 38 лет и был похоронен в местности каилдар, коунти каилдар, Ирландия.

sm.from 1327 to 1328: mai le 1327 e o'o I le 1328

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Fitz John Fitz Gerald 2nd Earl of Kildare nació en 1290, en Castletown, County Kildare, Irlanda, su papá, John Fitzthomas FITZGERALD 1st Earl of Kildare, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Blanche de la Roche, tenía 35 años. Se casó con Joan de Burgh el 16 de agosto de 1312, en Greencastle, County Down, Irlanda. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 9 de abril de 1328, en Maynooth, County Kildare, Irlanda, a la edad de 38 años, y fue sepultado en Kildare, County Kildare, Irlanda.
